openmeter/billing/charges/creditpurchase.purpose: The credit-purchase charge type within the billing/charges tagged-union family: defines the Charge/Intent/Settlement domain (this folder) and splits implementation across adapter/ (Ent persistence), service/ (transaction.Run orchestration + settlement dispatch), and lineengine/ (rating delegation). Primary constraint: all ledger side-effects flow through the creditpurchase.Handler interface so a noop handler can satisfy credits.enabled=false.

patterns.name: Settlement tagged-union via NewSettlement[T]
patterns.description: Settlement is a private-field struct with discriminator t; construct only via NewSettlement[T] and read via AsInvoiceSettlement/AsExternalSettlement/AsPromotionalSettlement. Never set t/invoice/external/promotional directly.
patterns.example: s := creditpurchase.NewSettlement(creditpurchase.ExternalSettlement{GenericSettlement: creditpurchase.GenericSettlement{Currency: "USD", CostBasis: amount}, InitialStatus: creditpurchase.CreatedInitialPaymentSettlementStatus})

patterns.name: Intent.Normalized() before Validate()
patterns.description: Always call input.Intent = input.Intent.Normalized() (truncates timestamps, rounds CreditAmount to currency precision) before input.Validate() in Create. The service mirrors this before reaching the adapter.
patterns.example:
input.Intent = input.Intent.Normalized()
if err := input.Validate(); err != nil { return err }

patterns.name: Handler mediation for all ledger interactions
patterns.description: Every ledger write goes through Handler methods (OnPromotionalCreditPurchase, OnCreditPurchaseInitiated, OnCreditPurchasePaymentAuthorized, OnCreditPurchasePaymentSettled). Promotional uses only OnPromotionalCreditPurchase; paid paths run Initiated -> Authorized -> Settled.
patterns.example: ref, err := h.handler.OnCreditPurchaseInitiated(ctx, charge)

patterns.name: models.NewNillableGenericValidationError in every Validate()
patterns.description: All input/domain Validate() methods collect into []error and return models.NewNillableGenericValidationError(errors.Join(errs...)) so nil is returned when empty. Never return raw fmt.Errorf from Validate().
patterns.example: return models.NewNillableGenericValidationError(errors.Join(errs...))

patterns.name: ValidationIssue sentinels with HTTP status
patterns.description: Domain conditions are package-level var ValidationIssue with an ErrCode constant and commonhttp.WithHTTPStatusCodeAttribute, matched upstream via errors.Is. Never invent a fresh error for a covered condition.
patterns.example: var ErrCreditPurchaseChargeNotActive = models.NewValidationIssue(ErrCodeCreditPurchaseChargeNotActive, "...", models.WithCriticalSeverity(), commonhttp.WithHTTPStatusCodeAttribute(http.StatusBadRequest))

patterns.name: Charge value-semantics for the generic state machine
patterns.description: Charge implements GetStatus/WithStatus/GetBase/WithBase as value receivers returning copies (meta.ChargeAccessor). Pointer-mutating these breaks the generic Machine external-storage pattern.
patterns.example: func (c Charge) WithStatus(status Status) Charge { c.Status = status; return c }

key_file_guides.role: Charge, ChargeBase, Intent, Realizations, State. Intent.Normalized() truncates timestamps and rounds CreditAmount.
key_file_guides.watch_for: CreditAmount must be positive; settlement currency must match credit currency; EffectiveAt is unsupported and always errors in Validate().

key_file_guides.role: Settlement tagged-union with JSON serde; three variants (Invoice/External/Promotional).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never instantiate Settlement{} directly; GetCostBasis() returns zero for promotional.

key_file_guides.role: Handler interface — sole abstraction for ledger side-effects, documenting the Initiated->Authorized->Settled happy path.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not call Handler methods outside the service layer.

key_file_guides.role: Service interface (CreditPurchaseService + ExternalPaymentLifecycle + InvoicePaymentLifecycle). Create returns ChargeWithGatheringLine.
key_file_guides.watch_for: GatheringLineToCreate may be non-nil; the parent charges.Service creates the gathering line after Create returns.

key_file_guides.role: Adapter (Charge+CreditGrant+ExternalPayment+InvoicedPayment + TxCreator) and input types, each with Validate().
key_file_guides.watch_for: Every new input type needs a Validate(); namespace must be non-empty.

key_file_guides.role: Status enum mirroring meta.ChargeStatus; ToMetaChargeStatus() converts back.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Use ToMetaChargeStatus() not a direct cast — direct cast skips Validate().

key_file_guides.role: ErrCreditPurchaseChargeNotActive sentinel (HTTP 400).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Match with errors.Is; do not create new errors for this condition.

anti_patterns: Calling ledger APIs directly instead of routing through creditpurchase.Handler — bypasses the credits.enabled=false noop guard.
Constructing Settlement{} via field assignment instead of NewSettlement[T] — leaves discriminator t empty.
Skipping intent.Normalized() before Validate() — denormalized timestamps and un-rounded amounts reach the adapter.
Returning raw fmt.Errorf from Validate() instead of models.NewNillableGenericValidationError — breaks error-type detection upstream.
Calling adapter methods outside transaction.Run in service code — breaks atomicity of multi-step charge flows.

decisions.decision: Settlement is a tagged union (private fields + discriminator) rather than an interface.
decisions.rationale: Enables exhaustive JSON serde and GetCostBasis() across variants without type assertions, and prevents partially-constructed Settlement values.

decisions.decision: Handler interface decouples ledger writes from charge service.
decisions.rationale: Allows a noop handler when credits.enabled=false without branching inside service code; keeps the ledger boundary clean.

decisions.decision: ChargeWithGatheringLine returned from Create rather than creating the line inline.
decisions.rationale: Gathering-line creation is deferred to the parent charges.Service to avoid a circular dependency between creditpurchase and billing.

code_examples.scenario: Create a credit purchase charge with invoice settlement

openmeter/billing/charges/lineage.purpose: Domain package for credit realization lineage tracking: defines Service and Adapter interfaces and the append-only Lineage/Segment model linking credit realizations to backing ledger transaction groups. Children split persistence (adapter/, Ent + FOR UPDATE locks) from orchestration (service/, transaction.RunWithNoValue). Primary constraint: segment mutation is close-then-split, never in-place update.

patterns.name: transaction.RunWithNoValue for multi-step mutations
patterns.description: Service methods calling multiple adapter methods wrap them in transaction.RunWithNoValue so all steps share the ctx-bound transaction. Calls outside the closure use a different connection and bypass the tx.
patterns.example: return transaction.RunWithNoValue(ctx, s.adapter, func(ctx context.Context) error { return s.adapter.CreateLineages(ctx, in) })

patterns.name: Input.Validate() before any business logic
patterns.description: Every service method calls input.Validate() first; this is separate from adapter-level validation and must not be skipped because lock/bulk-create paths do no further validation inside the transaction.
patterns.example: if err := input.Validate(); err != nil { return err }

patterns.name: Lock-then-mutate via FOR UPDATE
patterns.description: Adapter lock methods (LockCorrectionLineages, LockAdvanceLineagesForBackfill) take FOR UPDATE row locks inside an active tx and must be called before reading segments that will be mutated; the adapter guards with GetDriverFromContext.
patterns.example: lineages, err := s.adapter.LockCorrectionLineages(ctx, ns, realizationIDs)

patterns.name: Close-and-split for partial segment mutation
patterns.description: When only part of a segment amount is consumed, CloseSegment the existing row and CreateSegment two new rows rather than updating in place — append-only audit trail.
patterns.example: s.adapter.CloseSegment(ctx, seg.ID, closedAt); s.adapter.CreateSegment(ctx, consumedInput); s.adapter.CreateSegment(ctx, remainingInput)

patterns.name: clock.Now().Truncate(time.Microsecond) for segment timestamps
patterns.description: All closed_at timestamps truncate to microsecond precision to match Postgres storage and avoid equality-check failures. Note this differs from the meta package, which truncates to streaming.MinimumWindowSizeDuration.
patterns.example: closedAt := clock.Now().UTC().Truncate(time.Microsecond)

key_file_guides.role: Segment.Validate() with state-dependent rules, SortCorrectionPersistSegments and MinDecimal pure helpers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: BackingTransactionGroupID required for advance_backfilled and earnings_recognized segments; Segment.Amount must be positive.

key_file_guides.role: Service and Adapter interfaces plus all input types with Validate(). Lineage/Segment/ActiveSegmentsByRealizationID types.
key_file_guides.watch_for: CreateSegmentInput validation is state-dependent (source_state / backing_transaction_group_id requirements vary by State).

key_file_guides.role: Validates the source-backing-transaction-group rule for earnings_recognized segments sourced from advance_backfilled.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Mirror this table-driven validation style when adding new state combinations.

anti_patterns: Bypassing transaction.RunWithNoValue for multi-step adapter calls — creates partial writes when a step fails mid-sequence.
Calling adapter methods outside the RunWithNoValue closure — the closure's ctx carries the active tx; outside calls use a different connection.
Using clock.Now() without Truncate(time.Microsecond) for segment timestamps — Postgres precision mismatch fails equality checks.
Adding Ent query logic directly in service.go — all DB access goes through lineage.Adapter.
Removing the GetDriverFromContext guard from adapter lock methods — makes FOR UPDATE unreliable outside transactions.

decisions.decision: Close-and-split rather than update-in-place for segment mutation.
decisions.rationale: Append-only segment history enables auditing and prevents lost-update races under concurrent backfill.

decisions.decision: Lock operations require an active transaction (GetDriverFromContext guard).
decisions.rationale: FOR UPDATE locks are only meaningful inside a transaction; failing fast prevents silent non-locking reads.

decisions.decision: Package-level LoadActiveSegmentsByRealizationID alongside the adapter method.
decisions.rationale: Lets cross-package callers (creditpurchase service) load segments without a full lineage.Service dependency.

code_examples.scenario: Backfill advance lineage segments inside a transaction

openmeter/billing/charges/meta.purpose: Shared domain primitives for every charge type (flatfee, usagebased, creditpurchase): ChargeID, ChargeType, ChargeStatus, ManagedResource, the embedded Intent, Expands, the Patch interface (PatchDelete/Extend/Shrink backed by stateless.Trigger constants), and timestamp normalization. meta.Service is a type alias for meta.Adapter (a persistence-thin charge cross-reference registry, implemented in adapter/).

patterns.name: Patch interface with stateless.Trigger
patterns.description: Every patch implements Validate() + Op() PatchType + Trigger() stateless.Trigger + TriggerParams() any. Trigger constants live in triggers.go; never define trigger strings inline.
patterns.example:
func (p PatchExtend) Trigger() stateless.Trigger { return TriggerExtend }
func (p PatchExtend) TriggerParams() any { return p }

patterns.name: NormalizeTimestamp on all time fields
patterns.description: Timestamps in Intent and patches pass through NormalizeTimestamp (UTC + Truncate(streaming.MinimumWindowSizeDuration)) before persistence, via Intent.Normalized() or patch setters that normalize internally.
patterns.example: i.ServicePeriod = NormalizeClosedPeriod(i.ServicePeriod)

patterns.name: ValidateWith for period bounds checking
patterns.description: PatchExtend/PatchShrink expose ValidateWith(intent Intent) to check new periods against the existing intent; always call it in addition to Validate() when the existing intent is available.
patterns.example: if err := patch.ValidateWith(charge.Intent); err != nil { return err }

patterns.name: ChargeID = models.NamespacedID alias
patterns.description: ChargeID is a type alias of models.NamespacedID; validate with ChargeID.Validate() before reading Namespace/ID.
patterns.example: func (i ChargeID) Validate() error { return models.NamespacedID(i).Validate() }

patterns.name: Service = Adapter type alias
patterns.description: meta.Service is a type alias for meta.Adapter — no separate service layer exists. If orchestration is ever needed, remove the alias and add a real service with transaction.Run.
patterns.example: type Service = Adapter

key_file_guides.role: ChargeID, ChargeType, ChargeStatus, Expand/Expands, ChargeAccessor interface, Charge/Charges. ChargeAccessor requires GetChargeID/GetCustomerID/GetCurrency/ErrorAttributes.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ExpandNone is nil (not empty slice); every new charge type must implement ChargeAccessor.

key_file_guides.role: Shared Intent embedded in all charge intents; validates ManagedBy, CustomerID, Currency, three periods, TaxConfig, Subscription, UniqueReferenceID.
key_file_guides.watch_for: UniqueReferenceID must be nil or non-empty — empty string fails Validate().

key_file_guides.role: NormalizeTimestamp/NormalizeOptionalTimestamp/NormalizeClosedPeriod/Intent.Normalized(). Truncates to streaming.MinimumWindowSizeDuration.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Truncation precision is streaming.MinimumWindowSizeDuration, NOT time.Microsecond (differs from lineage).

key_file_guides.role: All stateless.Trigger constants shared across charge state machines.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Add new triggers here; never inline trigger strings in state-machine config files.

key_file_guides.role: PatchDelete + PatchDeletePolicy (Credit/Invoice refund policies). RefundAsCreditsDeletePolicy is the recommended default.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Both refund policies must be set; a zero-value policy fails Validate().

key_file_guides.role: Private-field patches with auto-normalizing setters and ValidateWith bounds checks; NewPatchExtend/NewPatchShrink constructors.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Use the constructors which validate before construction; setters normalize internally.

key_file_guides.role: meta.Adapter: RegisterCharges/DeleteRegisteredCharge + TxCreator. Implemented by adapter/ via TransactingRepoWithNoValue with soft-delete.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adapter is persistence-only; no business logic belongs here.

anti_patterns: Defining charge-type-specific copies of Intent period fields instead of embedding meta.Intent.
Calling NormalizeTimestamp with different precision than streaming.MinimumWindowSizeDuration.
Adding complex business logic to meta — it is a shared primitives package; logic belongs in type-specific service packages.
Using string literals for trigger constants instead of the vars in triggers.go.
Creating a PatchDelete with only one of the two refund policies set — the other defaults to empty and fails Validate().

decisions.decision: Service = Adapter type alias with no separate service layer.
decisions.rationale: meta is a persistence-thin registry; the alias avoids premature abstraction until orchestration is needed.

decisions.decision: Patch interface uses stateless.Trigger rather than a string enum.
decisions.rationale: Compile-time type safety with the stateless library; callers pass trigger + params to Machine.FireAndActivate without a string switch.
ClickHouse window aggregation needs consistent truncation granularity across all charge timestamps.

code_examples.scenario: Create and apply a PatchExtend with ValidateWith

openmeter/billing/charges/models.purpose: Structural parent grouping shared data-model sub-packages used by all charge types: chargemeta (Ent mixin + generic Create/Update/MapFromDB), creditrealization (allocation/correction ledger records + lineage specs), invoicedusage (accrued usage snapshots), ledgertransaction (GroupReference/TimedGroupReference), and payment (External/Invoiced payment models). No direct source files live here.

patterns.name: chargemeta.Mixin in all charge Ent schemas
patterns.description: Every charge Ent schema embeds chargemeta.Mixin via Mixin() to inherit standard charge columns (customer_id, periods, status, currency, managed_by, subscription refs, advance_after). Never re-define these per charge type.
patterns.example: func (CreditPurchaseCharge) Mixin() []ent.Mixin { return []ent.Mixin{chargemeta.Mixin{}, ...} }

patterns.name: creditrealization.Mixin requires SelfReferenceType
patterns.description: Entities holding credit realizations use creditrealization.Mixin with SelfReferenceType set to the entity's own type for self-referencing correction edges.
patterns.example: creditrealization.Mixin{SelfReferenceType: entschema.CreditPurchaseRealization{}}

patterns.name: payment.Base hierarchy for new payment variants
patterns.description: Payment entities share Base (status, currency, amount, authorized/settled refs) via payment.Mixin; Invoiced adds Immutable line_id/invoice_id. Never embed Base fields directly in a new variant.
patterns.example: type ExternalPayment struct { payment.Base; ... }

patterns.name: ValidationIssue sentinels in payment/errors.go
patterns.description: Payment domain errors are package-level var ValidationIssue with HTTP status codes, matched with errors.Is — not fmt.Errorf.
patterns.example: var ErrAlreadyAuthorized = models.NewValidationIssue(ErrCodeAlreadyAuthorized, "...", commonhttp.WithHTTPStatusCodeAttribute(http.StatusConflict))

patterns.name: ledgertransaction nil-safe helpers
patterns.description: Use GroupReference.GetIDOrNull() for Ent SetNillable* field methods; call Validate() before reading TransactionGroupID directly.
patterns.example: creator.SetNillableTransactionGroupID(ref.GetIDOrNull())

key_file_guides.role: Ent mixin with all standard charge columns plus generic Create/Update/MapFromDB helpers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: intent.Normalized() and meta.NormalizeOptionalTimestamp run at the top of Create/Update — never skip them; SortHint must be unique per batch.

key_file_guides.role: Realization/Realizations/InitialLineageSpec; allocation/correction duality enforced in CreateInput.Validate().
key_file_guides.watch_for: SortHint must be non-zero unique per batch for deterministic correction ordering.

key_file_guides.role: Realizations.Correct() correction planning.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Round amounts via currencyx.Calculator.RoundToPrecision before Correct(); never set CorrectsRealizationID manually.

key_file_guides.role: GroupReference and TimedGroupReference value types; GetIDOrNull() for nil-safe Ent writes.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Constructing TimedGroupReference with a zero Time fails Validate().

key_file_guides.role: Invoiced with Immutable LineID/InvoiceID.
key_file_guides.watch_for: LineID/InvoiceID must not be mutated after creation; UpdateInvoicedPayment is for status/transaction-group fields only.

key_file_guides.role: Domain payment error sentinels as ValidationIssue with HTTP status codes.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Use errors.Is; do not create new errors for already-covered conditions.

anti_patterns: Defining per-charge-type copies of chargemeta fields instead of embedding chargemeta.Mixin.
Setting SortHint=0 on all realizations in a batch — correction ordering becomes non-deterministic.
Accessing ledgertransaction.TransactionGroupID without calling Validate() first.
Mutating Invoiced.LineID or Invoiced.InvoiceID after creation — these are Immutable Ent fields.
Creating a correction realization without Realizations.Correct() — bypasses CorrectsRealizationID assignment and amount validation.

decisions.decision: chargemeta.Mixin + generic Create/Update/MapFromDB instead of per-type boilerplate.
decisions.rationale: Three charge types share identical persistence fields; type parameters eliminate duplication while keeping compile-time safety.

decisions.decision: Two-type realization model (allocation + correction) instead of mutable balance rows.
decisions.rationale: Append-only model provides a full audit trail for credit reconciliation and avoids lost-update races.

decisions.decision: Separate Invoiced and External payment types sharing a payment.Base.
decisions.rationale: Invoiced payments have an immutable invoice/line link not applicable to external payments; separate types prevent accidental mutation.

openmeter/billing/charges/worker.purpose: Organisational parent for charge-advancement workers split by execution model: advance/ for scheduled batch sweeps across all eligible customers (error accumulation + pagination) and asyncadvance/ for single-customer event-driven advancement triggered by a Watermill charges.AdvanceChargesEvent. Both children mutate only through charges.ChargeService, never Ent adapters directly.

patterns.name: Config struct + constructor validation
patterns.description: Each child defines a Config with required service fields and validates them in New*() before returning the concrete type; a nil ChargesService returns an error immediately.
patterns.example: func NewAdvancer(config Config) (*AutoAdvancer, error) { if config.ChargesService == nil { return nil, fmt.Errorf("charges service is required") }; return &AutoAdvancer{chargesService: config.ChargesService, logger: config.Logger}, nil }

patterns.name: Service-only access — no direct Ent calls
patterns.description: advance.AutoAdvancer and asyncadvance.Handler hold only a charges.ChargeService interface; Ent/adapter packages are never imported here.
patterns.example: type AutoAdvancer struct { chargesService charges.ChargeService; logger *slog.Logger }

patterns.name: Error accumulation in batch, pass-through in async
patterns.description: advance.All collects per-customer errors and returns errors.Join so one failure never blocks others; asyncadvance.Handle returns the error directly (single-customer scope) so Watermill can retry/DLQ.
patterns.example:
for _, cust := range customers { if err := a.AdvanceCharges(ctx, cust); err != nil { errs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf("customer %s: %w", cust.ID, err)) } }
return errors.Join(errs...)

patterns.name: Pagination via pagination.CollectAll
patterns.description: advance.ListCustomersToAdvance uses pagination.CollectAll with a pagination.NewPaginator closure to fetch the full customer list page-by-page without manual cursor management.
patterns.example: pagination.CollectAll(ctx, pagination.NewPaginator(func(ctx context.Context, page pagination.Page) (pagination.Result[customer.CustomerID], error) { return a.chargesService.ListCustomersToAdvance(ctx, ...) }), defaultPageSize)

patterns.name: Stable now captured once per batch
patterns.description: Time is captured once at the top of All() and passed to ListCustomersToAdvance — never re-captured inside the paginator closure or per-customer calls.
patterns.example: now := clock.Now(); customers, err := a.ListCustomersToAdvance(ctx, now)

key_file_guides.role: Batch sweeper: paginated list of eligible customers, per-customer advance, error accumulation across customers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: time.Now() is captured once at the top of All() — do not capture inside the paginator closure or per-customer calls.

key_file_guides.role: Single-customer Watermill handler: builds customer.CustomerID from event fields and calls AdvanceCharges. Exactly one public method Handle(ctx, *charges.AdvanceChargesEvent) error.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Return the AdvanceCharges error — never return nil to suppress retries; Watermill uses it for retry/DLQ routing.

anti_patterns: Calling Ent/adapter code directly — all mutations must go through charges.ChargeService.
Returning on first customer error in batch paths — must accumulate with errors.Join.
Introducing context.Background()/context.TODO() — always propagate the caller's ctx.
Adding batch/pagination logic to asyncadvance.Handler — that belongs in advance.AutoAdvancer.
Returning nil from asyncadvance.Handle to swallow failures — Watermill uses the error for retry/DLQ routing.

decisions.decision: Separate packages for batch (advance) and async (asyncadvance) advance paths.
decisions.rationale: Execution models differ: batch sweeps all customers with pagination and error accumulation; async handles one Watermill event with pass-through error semantics. Mixing them couples scheduling and event-bus concerns.

decisions.decision: Error accumulation via errors.Join in the batch path instead of early return.
decisions.rationale: One failing customer must not prevent others from advancing; all failures surface together for observability and retry.

code_examples.scenario: Construct and run the batch sweeper
